title: Global Awards & Fellowships
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
Global Awards & Fellowships
Global awards are competitive, merit-based external scholarships and fellowships that allow motivated students to pursue advanced research, service or leadership goals. Through one-on-one advising, students identify opportunities of interest, prepare applications, undergo intensive training and apply for some of the world's most competitive postgraduate opportunities.
Ready to start your journey?
Latest Updates
The Office of Global Awards is delighted to congratulate NYU Shanghai’s newest Schwarzman Scholars: congratulations to Yijia Cao '26, NYU Shanghai senior, and alumnus Stephanie Anderson '23!
Global Awards & Fellowship News
Annie Church ’26 Awarded Fulbright ETA Annie Church ’26 has been awarded a Fulbright English Teaching Assistantship. Read more
Annie Church ’26 Awarded Fulbright ETA
Annie Church ’26 has been awarded a Fulbright English Teaching Assistantship.
Record Number of Students Win Erasmus Mundus Scholarships A record number of students have been selected for the Erasmus Mundus Scholarship program. Read more
Record Number of Students Win Erasmus Mundus Scholarships
A record number of students have been selected for the Erasmus Mundus Scholarship program.
Two Alumni Named as Yenching Scholars NYU Shanghai alumni Maylee Sexton ’25 and Christina Bowllan ’22 have been named as Yenching Scholars. Read more
Two Alumni Named as Yenching Scholars
NYU Shanghai alumni Maylee Sexton ’25 and Christina Bowllan ’22 have been named as Yenching Scholars.
Celebrate Global Awards at 10 Years
Meet some of our Global Awards alumni from the past ten years! Learn more about the passions that drove them and the moments that have shaped their paths.
What Next?
Browse some of the scholarships and fellowships that NYU advises on or administers and begin to conduct your own research beyond these lists.
Thinking of applying for a postgraduate fellowship this year or next? Email shanghai.global.awards@nyu.edu for an up-to-date advising pack.
To make an appointment for Global Awards advising at any point during your NYU Shanghai career, please contact: shanghai.global.awards@nyu.edu
Anna Kathryn Kendrick, PhDDirector of Global Awards
Steve IamsPhDAssistant Dean of Academic AffairsGlobal Awards Faculty Advisor
Ying ChenAcademic Affairs and Global Awards Coordinator
